questions.question: What is centripetal acceleration?
choices: Acceleration directed away from the center
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration is always directed toward the center of the circle.

questions.question: Which equation gives the magnitude of centripetal acceleration?
A 2 kg object moves in a circle of radius 4 m at 6 m/s. What is the centripetal force?
What is the period of an object moving at 8 m/s in a circle of radius 4 m?
If the speed of an object in uniform circular motion doubles, what happens to the centripetal acceleration?
questions.explanation: a_c = v^2/r, so if v doubles, a_c increases by a factor of 4.

questions.question: What is the direction of the velocity vector in uniform circular motion?
questions.explanation: The velocity vector is always tangent to the circle.
